🤖 AI Health Insights

Health Alert82% confidence

Elevated ALT Requires Follow-up

Luna's liver enzyme (ALT) was 89 U/L in her November bloodwork — 62% above the upper normal limit. While not yet at a critical level, this warrants monitoring. The most likely cause is her new Omega-3 supplement, which can occasionally affect liver enzymes. I recommend scheduling a recheck panel in February as Dr. Mitchell suggested, and temporarily pausing the supplement 2 weeks before the test for a cleaner baseline.

November 2025 bloodworkOmega-3 supplement started Sept 2025
Recommendation91% confidence

Dental Health Needs Attention

Based on Dr. Mitchell's notes from Luna's wellness visit, early tartar buildup was observed. Golden Retrievers are prone to periodontal disease, which can affect heart and kidney health if untreated. At age 4, this is the ideal time for a professional cleaning before issues become serious. I'd recommend booking an appointment within the next 2-3 months and adding daily dental chews as a preventive measure.

Wellness visit Nov 2025Breed health profile: Golden Retriever
